title: SofaBuffers Generator Config Schema
description: Validates sofabgen configuration files (YAML or JSON). PLAN §7. Every level is closed (additionalProperties:false) so typos are rejected, not ignored. Every key in this schema is honored by the generator; the schema and the set of consumed keys are kept in lockstep.

emit.description: sources (default): just the message-type source files. project: additionally scaffold a buildable project (build files + the canonical-JSON conformance harness).

namespace.description: Namespace wrapping the generated types, for the targets that have one (C++ namespace, C# namespace). No generic default on purpose: each backend applies its own idiomatic default (C++ `message`, C# `Message`).

input_dir.description: Directory of message definitions to generate from. The CLI --in flag overrides it.

output_dir.description: Directory the generated files are written to. The CLI --out flag overrides it.

tool_banner.description: Tool name stamped into every generated file header (default: sofabgen).

license.description: SPDX license identifier stamped into every generated file header (e.g. MIT, Apache-2.0, LicenseRef-Acme). Unset or `none` (the default) emits no SPDX line.

symbol_prefix.description: Prefix applied to every generated C symbol - struct typedefs, descriptor tables, and the encode/decode/init functions (default: message_).

namespace.description: C++ namespace wrapping the generated types (default: message). Overrides generic.namespace for this target.

corelib.description: Which C++ corelib the generated code targets. cpp (default): pure-C++20 header-only corelib-cpp with dynamic containers (std::vector/std::string). c-cpp: the C++ wrapper over corelib-c-cpp with fixed-capacity, heap-free containers sized from the schema.

allow_dynamic.description: corelib: c-cpp only. Keep a std::vector/std::string heap fallback for genuinely unbounded fields (string/blob without maxlen, array without count) instead of failing generation; bounded fields still use fixed-capacity inline storage.

corelib.description: Which Rust corelib the generated crate targets. rs (default): the std corelib-rs, tuned for throughput. rs-no-std: the #![no_std] corelib-rs-no-std, feature-gated per wire type and tuned for footprint.

no_std.description: rust corelib: rs-no-std only. Emit a genuinely #![no_std], heap-free crate: heapless::String<N>/Vec<T,N> fixed-capacity fields sized from the schema, a bounded decode stack, and serde gated behind a cargo feature. On by default for corelib: rs-no-std; set false to emit an ordinary std crate against the no-std corelib.

allow_dynamic.description: rust no_std only. Keep an alloc::String/alloc::Vec heap fallback for genuinely unbounded fields (string/blob without maxlen, array without count) instead of failing generation; bounded fields still use fixed-capacity heapless storage. The Rust analog of the c-cpp allow_dynamic.

package.description: The `package <name>` clause of the generated .go files (default: message).

module_path.description: The module path written to the generated go.mod in project mode (default: example.com/generated).

go_version.description: The `go <version>` directive written to the generated go.mod in project mode (default: 1.21).

package.description: The `package <name>;` declaration of the generated classes, and the Maven source-directory layout in project mode (default: message).

namespace.description: The `namespace <name>` wrapping the generated classes (default: Message). Overrides generic.namespace for this target.

int64.description: 64-bit field representation. bigint (default): bigint scalars and bigint[] arrays. long: u64[]/i64[] backed by corelib Long[] behind a get/set accessor pair (bigint-free encode/decode hot path; assignment accepts Long | bigint | number); scalars stay bigint. number: additionally maps u64/i64 scalars to number - the caller guarantees scalar values fit the +/-2^53 safe-integer range. All modes are wire-identical.

format.description: Documentation output format. html (default, and currently the only format): a single self-contained page (inline CSS, no external assets) documenting every message and named type.
